SEARCH
Package com.hotelreservation.gateway.filter

Class AuthenticationFilter

java.lang.Object
org.springframework.cloud.gateway.support.AbstractConfigurable<AuthenticationFilter.Config>
org.springframework.cloud.gateway.filter.factory.AbstractGatewayFilterFactory<AuthenticationFilter.Config>
com.hotelreservation.gateway.filter.AuthenticationFilter
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.springframework.beans.factory.Aware, org.springframework.cloud.gateway.filter.factory.GatewayFilterFactory<AuthenticationFilter.Config>, org.springframework.cloud.gateway.support.Configurable<AuthenticationFilter.Config>, org.springframework.cloud.gateway.support.ShortcutConfigurable, org.springframework.context.ApplicationEventPublisherAware
@Component public class AuthenticationFilter extends org.springframework.cloud.gateway.filter.factory.AbstractGatewayFilterFactory<AuthenticationFilter.Config>
API Gateway filter that enforces JWT authentication on protected routes and forwards user context headers to downstream microservices.

This is the single centralised authentication enforcement point for the entire system. All protected routes in application.properties reference this filter by name (AuthenticationFilter). Downstream services receive pre-validated user context headers and do not need to re-query the database.

Filter responsibilities:

  1. Reject any request missing a valid Authorization: Bearer header with 401 Unauthorized.
  2. Parse and validate the JWT signature and expiry using the shared secret.
  3. Extract custom JWT claims (userId, roles) and the subject (username) from the token.
  4. Mutate the forwarded request to add three context headers for downstream use:
    • X-Username — the authenticated user's username
    • X-User-Id — the authenticated user's database ID
    • X-Roles — comma-separated list of the user's role names

This pattern (gateway-level auth + header forwarding) avoids redundant database calls in each microservice while still allowing method-level @PreAuthorize role checks in the downstream controllers.

  • Constructor Details

    • AuthenticationFilter

      public AuthenticationFilter()
      Constructs the filter and registers it with the gateway filter factory. The AuthenticationFilter.Config class is passed to the parent to support YAML/properties configuration.

  • Method Details

    • apply

      public org.springframework.cloud.gateway.filter.GatewayFilter apply(AuthenticationFilter.Config config)
      Builds and returns the reactive GatewayFilter logic for this factory.

      The returned filter is applied to every route that includes filters: AuthenticationFilter in the gateway route configuration.

      Processing flow for each request:

      1. Read the Authorization header from the incoming request.
      2. Reject immediately with 401 if the header is missing or not a Bearer token.
      3. Parse the JWT and validate signature + expiry using the shared HMAC secret.
      4. Extract sub (username), userId, and roles claims.
      5. Mutate the request to include X-Username, X-User-Id, and X-Roles headers before forwarding to the downstream microservice.
      6. On any JWT error (expired, malformed, wrong signature), reject with 401.
      Parameters:
      config - the filter configuration (currently unused — no config properties needed)
      Returns:
      the configured GatewayFilter instance
